name: Load Balancing
description: A load balancer spreads incoming requests across backend servers: it selects a target by an algorithm such as round-robin, least-connections, or weighted hashing, checks backend health and removes unhealthy nodes from the pool, then forwards the request, achieving an even and resilient distribution of load.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Incoming requests"]
    N2["Load balancer receives"]
    N3{"Choose algorithm"}
    N4["Round-robin"]
    N5["Least connections"]
    N6["Weighted / hash"]
    N7["Health-check backends"]
    N8{"Backend healthy?"}
    N9["Remove from pool"]
    N10["Forward to server"]
    N11["Balanced distribution"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|round-robin| N4
    N3 -->|least-conn| N5
    N3 -->|weighted| N6
    N4 --> N7
    N5 --> N7
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|recheck| N7
    N8 -->|yes| N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
